assertion.text: Erdős Problem #90: declared status 'disproved'. Formalized: yes. Does every set of $n$ distinct points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ contain at most $n^{1+O(1/\log\log n)}$ many pairs which are distance 1 apart? Current best: In \cite{Er82e} he offers \$300 for the upper bound $n^{1+o(1)}$. This would be the best possible, as is shown by a set of lattice points. The best known upper bound is $O(n^{4/3})$, due to Spencer, Szemer\'{e}di, and Trotter \cite{SST84}. In \cite{Er83c} and \cite{Er85} Erd\H{o}s offers \$250 for an upper bound of the form $n^{1+o(1)}$. Prize: $500. OEIS: A186705. Tags: distances, geometry.
